content: What did the angel tell Joseph to name the child?
explanation: Scripture says: 'You shall call his name Jesus' (Matthew 1:21).

content: Where was Jesus born?
explanation: Scripture says: 'Jesus was born in Bethlehem' (Matthew 2:1).

content: When Jesus was baptized, in what form did the Spirit descend?
explanation: Scripture says: 'The Spirit of God descended on Him like a dove' (Matthew 3:16).

content: Jesus answered Satan, 'Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that comes from the mouth of God,' when tempted to do what?
explanation: Scripture says: 'If you are the Son of God, command these stones to become loaves of bread' (Matthew 4:3).
options: Throw himself from the temple pinnacle

content: What is the first beatitude in the Sermon on the Mount?
explanation: Scripture says: 'Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven' (Matthew 5:3).
options: Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness
Blessed are the poor in spirit

content: How does the Lord's Prayer 'Our Father in heaven' continue?
explanation: Scripture says: 'Our Father in heaven, hallowed be your name. Your kingdom come, your will be done' (Matthew 6:9-10).

content: What does the name 'Immanuel' mean?
explanation: Scripture says: 'Immanuel, which means, God with us' (Matthew 1:23).

content: What did the wise men offer to Jesus?
explanation: Scripture says: 'They opened their treasures and offered him gifts: gold, frankincense, and myrrh' (Matthew 2:11).

content: When Jesus was baptized, what did the voice from heaven say?
explanation: Scripture says: 'A voice from heaven said: This is my beloved Son, with whom I am well pleased' (Matthew 3:17).

content: Jesus said to Simon and Andrew, 'Follow me, and I will make you become ___'
explanation: Scripture says: 'Follow me, and I will make you fishers of men' (Matthew 4:19).

content: Jesus says the disciples are what to the world?
explanation: Scripture says: 'You are the salt of the earth... You are the light of the world' (Matthew 5:13-14).

content: Jesus said the birds of the air do not sow, reap, or store in barns, but who feeds them?
explanation: Scripture says: 'Look at the birds of the air: they neither sow nor reap nor gather into barns, and yet your heavenly Father feeds them' (Matthew 6:26).

content: Jesus said, 'Whatever you wish that others would do to you, do also to them.' What is this called?
explanation: Scripture says: 'Whatever you wish that others would do to you, do also to them, for this is the Law and the Prophets' (Matthew 7:12).

content: When the storm rose on the sea, whom did Jesus rebuke?
explanation: Scripture says: 'Then he rose and rebuked the winds and the sea, and there was a great calm' (Matthew 8:26).

content: Jesus said the Son of Man has what authority on earth?
explanation: Scripture says: 'That you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins' (Matthew 9:6).

content: Jesus said do not fear those who can kill the body but cannot kill the soul. Whom did He warn against?
explanation: Scripture says: 'Do not fear those who kill the body but cannot kill the soul' (Matthew 10:28).

content: Jesus said He came to do what concerning the Law?
explanation: Scripture says: 'I have not come to abolish the Law, but to fulfill it' (Matthew 5:17).

content: Whom does Jesus invite to come to Him?
explanation: Scripture says: 'Come to me, all who labor and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest' (Matthew 11:28).
options: All who labor and are heavy laden

content: On what did Jesus say He would build His church?
explanation: Scripture says: 'On this rock I will build my church' — referring to Peter's confession (Matthew 16:18).

content: In whose name did Jesus command His disciples to baptize?
explanation: Scripture says: 'Go therefore and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit' (Matthew 28:19).
options: The Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit

content: The angel told Joseph to name the child Jesus (Matthew 1:21). What does the Hebrew name 'Jesus' mean?
explanation: Yeshua (ישוע) means 'the LORD saves' — the name reflects Jesus' mission.

content: In the Sermon on the Mount, Jesus said 'Blessed are the poor in spirit' (Matthew 5:3). What does the Greek word for 'poor' mean?
explanation: Ptochos (πτωχός) means 'poor in spirit' — recognizing one's need before God.

content: What does the name 'Jesus' (Yeshua) mean?
explanation: Matthew 1:21 — Jesus (Yeshua) means 'the LORD saves.' His name reveals His purpose for coming.

content: At Jesus' baptism, how were all three Persons of the Trinity present?
explanation: Matthew 3:16-17 — Jesus (the Son) was baptized, the Holy Spirit descended like a dove, and the Father said: 'This is my beloved Son.'
options: Only the Son and the Father
The Son was baptized, the Spirit descended like a dove, the Father spoke from heaven

content: When tempted by Satan to turn stones into bread, what did Jesus answer?
explanation: Matthew 4:4 — Jesus used the Word of God (Deuteronomy 8:3) to defeat every temptation of Satan.
options: You shall not tempt the Lord
Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that comes from the mouth of God

content: The Beatitudes begin: 'Blessed are those who are ___'
explanation: Matthew 5:3 — Blessed are those who recognize their spiritual poverty, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.

content: What does Jesus teach about enemies?
explanation: Matthew 5:44 — This is one of Jesus' most exalted teachings, transcending ordinary morality.
options: Love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you

content: How does Jesus' model prayer begin?
explanation: Matthew 6:9 — Jesus teaches us to address God as Father, expressing intimacy with Him.

content: Jesus speaks of two ways. To what does the narrow gate lead?
explanation: Matthew 7:14 — The narrow gate and hard way lead to life, and few find it. The wide gate and easy way lead to destruction.

content: Peter declared about Jesus: 'You are ___'
explanation: Matthew 16:16 — This is Peter's most important confession of faith, commended by Jesus.
options: The Christ, the Son of the living God

content: Which commandment did Jesus say was the greatest?
explanation: Matthew 22:37-38 — Loving God with all one's being is the greatest, and loving one's neighbor as oneself is the second.
options: Love the Lord with all your heart, soul, and mind

content: What is Jesus' Great Commission?
explanation: Matthew 28:19 — Go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit.
options: Go and make disciples of all nations

content: Jesus invites: 'Come to me, all who labor and are heavy laden, and I will give you ___'
explanation: Matthew 11:28 — Jesus invites everyone weighed down to come to Him for true rest.

content: Peter asked how many times to forgive. What did Jesus answer?
explanation: Matthew 18:22 — Forgiveness has no limit. Seventy times seven means always forgive.

content: Matthew opens with a genealogy of Jesus as the son of which two patriarchs?
explanation: Matthew 1:1 — 'The book of the genealogy of Jesus Christ, the son of David, the son of Abraham.'

content: In the dream, the angel explained Mary conceived by whom?
explanation: Matthew 1:20 — 'Joseph, son of David, do not fear to take Mary as your wife, for that which is conceived in her is from the Holy Spirit.'

content: After the Magi left, Joseph was told to take the child to where?
explanation: Matthew 2:13 — 'Rise, take the child and his mother, and flee to Egypt, and remain there until I tell you.'

content: King Herod ordered the killing of children in Bethlehem of what age?
explanation: Matthew 2:16 — 'He sent and killed all the male children in Bethlehem and in all that region who were two years old or under.'

content: Returning from Egypt, the family settled in which city, fulfilling 'He shall be called a Nazarene'?
explanation: Matthew 2:23 — 'And he went and lived in a city called Nazareth, so that what was spoken by the prophets might be fulfilled, that he would be called a Nazarene.'

content: Where did John the Baptist preach, and what was his main message?
explanation: Matthew 3:1-2 — 'In those days John the Baptist came preaching in the wilderness of Judea, Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand.'
options: In the wilderness of Judea: Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand
In the temple: Keep the law
On the mountain: Follow the Messiah

content: What did John the Baptist wear and eat?
explanation: Matthew 3:4 — 'Now John wore a garment of camel's hair and a leather belt around his waist, and his food was locusts and wild honey.'
options: Camel's hair garment, leather belt; locusts and wild honey

content: John said the One coming after him would baptize with what?
explanation: Matthew 3:11 — 'He who is coming after me... will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire.'
options: With the Holy Spirit and fire

content: Jesus was tempted in the wilderness after fasting how many days and nights?
explanation: Matthew 4:2 — 'And after fasting forty days and forty nights, he was hungry.'

content: Jesus answered the second temptation (jumping from the temple) with which Scripture?
explanation: Matthew 4:7 — 'Jesus said to him, Again it is written, You shall not put the Lord your God to the test.' (quoting Deuteronomy 6:16)
options: Man shall not live by bread alone
You shall fear the LORD your God
You shall not put the Lord your God to the test
You shall love the Lord with all your heart

content: Jesus traveled throughout Galilee doing what three main things?
explanation: Matthew 4:23 — 'And he went throughout all Galilee, teaching in their synagogues and proclaiming the gospel of the kingdom and healing every disease and every affliction among the people.'

content: In the Sermon on the Mount, who shall inherit the earth?
explanation: Matthew 5:5 — 'Blessed are the meek, for they shall inherit the earth.'

content: Blessed are the pure in heart — for they shall do what?
explanation: Matthew 5:8 — 'Blessed are the pure in heart, for they shall see God.'

content: Jesus called the disciples what 'of the earth'?
explanation: Matthew 5:13 — 'You are the salt of the earth, but if salt has lost its taste, how shall its saltiness be restored?'

content: When someone slaps you on the right cheek, what does Jesus teach?
explanation: Matthew 5:39 — 'But I say to you, Do not resist the one who is evil. But if anyone slaps you on the right cheek, turn to him the other also.'

content: Where does Jesus teach believers should pray?
explanation: Matthew 6:6 — 'But when you pray, go into your room and shut the door and pray to your Father who is in secret.'
options: On the street for all to see
In your room, with the door shut, in secret

content: According to Matthew 6:14-15, what is the condition for the Father to forgive us?
explanation: Matthew 6:14-15 — 'For if you forgive others their trespasses, your heavenly Father will also forgive you, but if you do not forgive others their trespasses, neither will your Father forgive your trespasses.'

content: Jesus said you cannot serve God and what?
explanation: Matthew 6:24 — 'No one can serve two masters... You cannot serve God and money [Mammon].'

content: Matthew 6:33 — Seek first what?
explanation: Matthew 6:33 — 'But seek first the kingdom of God and his righteousness, and all these things will be added to you.'
options: The kingdom of God and His righteousness

content: Matthew 7:1 teaches us not to do what to others?
explanation: Matthew 7:1 — 'Judge not, that you be not judged.'
options: Judge not, that you be not judged

content: Matthew 7:7 — Three commands of Jesus: '___, ___, ___'
explanation: Matthew 7:7 — 'Ask, and it will be given to you; seek, and you will find; knock, and it will be opened to you.'

content: In the closing parable of the Sermon on the Mount, the wise man built his house on what?
explanation: Matthew 7:24-25 — 'Everyone then who hears these words of mine and does them will be like a wise man who built his house on the rock.'

content: Jesus said He had not found such great faith — even in Israel — in whom?
explanation: Matthew 8:10 — A Roman centurion at Capernaum. 'Truly, I tell you, with no one in Israel have I found such faith.'

content: Jesus said: 'The Son of Man has nowhere to ___'
explanation: Matthew 8:20 — 'Foxes have holes, and birds of the air have nests, but the Son of Man has nowhere to lay his head.'

content: At the tax booth, whom did Jesus call to follow Him?
explanation: Matthew 9:9 — 'As Jesus passed on from there, he saw a man called Matthew sitting at the tax booth, and he said to him, Follow me. And he rose and followed him.'

content: Jesus saw the crowds as sheep without a shepherd; what did He tell the disciples to do?
explanation: Matthew 9:38 — 'Therefore pray earnestly to the Lord of the harvest to send out laborers into his harvest.'
options: Find a quiet place to rest
Pray the Lord of the harvest to send laborers

content: How many apostles did Jesus choose according to Matthew 10:1-4?
explanation: Matthew 10:1-4 — Twelve apostles listed: Simon (Peter), Andrew, James, John, Philip, Bartholomew, Thomas, Matthew, James son of Alphaeus, Thaddaeus, Simon the Zealot, Judas Iscariot.

content: Jesus said He came not to bring what, but a sword?
explanation: Matthew 10:34 — 'Do not think that I have come to bring peace to the earth. I have not come to bring peace, but a sword.'

content: Jesus answered John the Baptist's disciples by listing what miracles?
explanation: Matthew 11:5 — 'The blind receive their sight and the lame walk, lepers are cleansed and the deaf hear, and the dead are raised up, and the poor have good news preached to them.'
options: Blind see, lame walk, lepers cleansed, deaf hear, dead raised, poor hear gospel

content: Jesus said no one born of woman is greater than John the Baptist; but in the kingdom of heaven what?
explanation: Matthew 11:11 — 'Truly, I say to you, among those born of women there has arisen no one greater than John the Baptist. Yet the one who is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.'
options: The least in the kingdom is greater than he
No one is equal to John

content: When the disciples plucked grain on the Sabbath, what did Jesus claim to be Lord of?
explanation: Matthew 12:8 — 'For the Son of Man is lord of the Sabbath.'

content: What sin will not be forgiven in this age or the age to come?
explanation: Matthew 12:31-32 — 'Therefore I tell you, every sin and blasphemy will be forgiven people, but the blasphemy against the Spirit will not be forgiven.'

content: Matthew 12:34 — Out of what fullness of the heart does the mouth speak?
explanation: Matthew 12:34 — 'Out of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaks.'
options: Out of the abundance of the heart, the mouth speaks

content: Jesus compared His three days in the heart of the earth with whom in the belly of the fish?
explanation: Matthew 12:40 — 'For just as Jonah was three days and three nights in the belly of the great fish, so will the Son of Man be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth.'

content: In the parable of the sower, the seed fell on what four kinds of soil?
explanation: Matthew 13:4-8 — 'Some seeds fell along the path... other seeds fell on rocky ground... other seeds fell among thorns... other seeds fell on good soil.'
options: Along the path, rocky ground, among thorns, good soil

content: Jesus compared the kingdom of heaven to which seed, smallest of all seeds?
explanation: Matthew 13:31-32 — 'The kingdom of heaven is like a grain of mustard seed that a man took and sowed in his field. It is the smallest of all seeds...'

content: In the parable, what did the man find hidden in a field that he sold all to buy the field for?
explanation: Matthew 13:44 — 'The kingdom of heaven is like treasure hidden in a field, which a man found and covered up. Then in his joy he goes and sells all that he has and buys that field.'

content: How many loaves and fish did Jesus multiply to feed the 5,000?
explanation: Matthew 14:17-21 — 'They said to him, We have only five loaves here and two fish... And those who ate were about five thousand men, besides women and children.'

content: Why did Peter sink while walking on water?
explanation: Matthew 14:30-31 — 'When he saw the wind, he was afraid, and beginning to sink he cried out, Lord, save me. Jesus immediately reached out his hand and took hold of him, saying to him, O you of little faith, why did you doubt?'
options: He was afraid and had little faith (doubted)

content: How did Jesus describe the Canaanite woman's faith?
explanation: Matthew 15:28 — 'Then Jesus answered her, O woman, great is your faith! Be it done for you as you desire. And her daughter was healed instantly.'

content: In the second feeding miracle, Jesus fed about how many people?
explanation: Matthew 15:38 — 'Those who ate were four thousand men, besides women and children.'

content: Jesus taught that whoever wants to follow Him must do three things: deny himself, take up what, and follow Him?
explanation: Matthew 16:24 — 'If anyone would come after me, let him deny himself and take up his cross and follow me.'

content: Which three disciples did Jesus take up the mountain for the Transfiguration?
explanation: Matthew 17:1 — 'And after six days Jesus took with him Peter and James, and John his brother, and led them up a high mountain by themselves.'

content: At the Transfiguration, what did the voice from the cloud say?
explanation: Matthew 17:5 — 'This is my beloved Son, with whom I am well pleased; listen to him.'
options: This is my beloved Son, with whom I am well pleased; listen to Him

content: Jesus said if you have faith like which seed, you can move mountains?
explanation: Matthew 17:20 — 'For truly, I say to you, if you have faith like a grain of mustard seed, you will say to this mountain, Move from here to there, and it will move.'

content: Jesus said unless you become like whom, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven?
explanation: Matthew 18:3 — 'Unless you turn and become like children, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.'

content: Jesus promised His presence among how many gathered in His name?
explanation: Matthew 18:20 — 'For where two or three are gathered in my name, there am I among them.'

content: In the parable of the unforgiving servant, how much debt did the king forgive?
explanation: Matthew 18:24 — 'When he began to settle, one was brought to him who owed him ten thousand talents.' This represents an enormous, billion-dollar-equivalent debt.

content: Jesus said: 'Let the children come to me; do not hinder them, for to such belongs ___'
explanation: Matthew 19:14 — 'For to such belongs the kingdom of heaven.'

content: What did Jesus tell the rich young man to do to be perfect?
explanation: Matthew 19:21 — 'If you would be perfect, go, sell what you possess and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; and come, follow me.'
options: Sell all you have, give to the poor, then follow Me
Go to the temple and sacrifice

content: Jesus said it is easier for what animal to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter the kingdom?
explanation: Matthew 19:24 — 'It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter the kingdom of God.'

content: In the parable of the laborers in the vineyard, who received equal pay despite working different hours?
explanation: Matthew 20:9-12 — 'The master paid each one a denarius, whether they worked from the first hour or the eleventh.' Lesson: in God's kingdom, the master gives by grace, not by merit.
options: All workers, each one a denarius

content: Matthew 20:28 — The Son of Man came not to be served, but to do what?
explanation: Matthew 20:28 — 'Even as the Son of Man came not to be served but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.'
options: To serve, and to give His life as a ransom for many

content: Jesus entered Jerusalem riding on what?
explanation: Matthew 21:7 — 'They brought the donkey and the colt and put on them their cloaks, and he sat on them.' Fulfilling Zechariah 9:9.

content: Entering the temple, Jesus rebuked them for making the house of prayer into what?
explanation: Matthew 21:13 — 'It is written, My house shall be called a house of prayer, but you make it a den of robbers.'

content: Jesus quoted Psalm 118: 'The stone that the builders rejected has become ___'
explanation: Matthew 21:42 — 'The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone; this was the Lord's doing, and it is marvelous in our eyes.'

content: When the Pharisees asked about paying taxes to Caesar, what did Jesus say?
explanation: Matthew 22:21 — 'Therefore render to Caesar the things that are Caesar's, and to God the things that are God's.'
options: Render to Caesar the things of Caesar, and to God the things of God

content: Matthew 23:23 — Jesus rebuked the scribes for tithing mint, dill, and cumin, but neglecting what?
explanation: Matthew 23:23 — 'Woe to you... For you tithe mint and dill and cumin, and have neglected the weightier matters of the law: justice and mercy and faithfulness.'

content: According to Matthew 24:14, what will be preached throughout the world before the end comes?
explanation: Matthew 24:14 — 'And this gospel of the kingdom will be proclaimed throughout the whole world as a testimony to all nations, and then the end will come.'

content: Jesus said heaven and earth will pass away, but what will not pass away?
explanation: Matthew 24:35 — 'Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away.'

content: In the parable of the ten virgins, what did the five wise prepare that the five foolish did not?
explanation: Matthew 25:4 — 'But the wise took flasks of oil with their lamps.'

content: In the parable of the talents, how many talents did the master give to the three servants?
explanation: Matthew 25:15 — 'To one he gave five talents, to another two, to another one, to each according to his ability. Then he went away.'

content: In the final judgment, the King says: 'As you did it to one of the ___ of these my brothers, you did it to me.'
explanation: Matthew 25:40 — 'Truly, I say to you, as you did it to one of the least of these my brothers, you did it to me.'

content: In Gethsemane, Jesus prayed: 'My Father, if it be possible, let this cup pass from me; nevertheless, not as I will, but as ___'
explanation: Matthew 26:39 — 'My Father, if it be possible, let this cup pass from me; nevertheless, not as I will, but as you will.'

content: Judas Iscariot betrayed Jesus for how many pieces of silver?
explanation: Matthew 26:15 — 'And they paid him thirty pieces of silver.' Fulfilling Zechariah 11:12-13.

content: After the rooster crowed, what did Peter do?
explanation: Matthew 26:75 — 'And Peter remembered the saying of Jesus, Before the rooster crows, you will deny me three times. And he went out and wept bitterly.'

content: When Jesus yielded up His spirit, what happened to the temple curtain?
explanation: Matthew 27:51 — 'And behold, the curtain of the temple was torn in two, from top to bottom. And the earth shook, and the rocks were split.'
options: It was torn in two from top to bottom

content: After Jesus died, what did the Roman centurion confess?
explanation: Matthew 27:54 — 'When the centurion and those who were with him, keeping watch over Jesus, saw the earthquake and what took place, they were filled with awe and said, Truly this was the Son of God!'
options: This was the king of the Jews
Truly this was the Son of God

content: At the empty tomb, what did the angel say to the women about Jesus?
explanation: Matthew 28:6 — 'He is not here, for he has risen, as he said. Come, see the place where he lay.'
options: He is not here; He has risen, as He said

content: After His resurrection, Jesus declared He has what 'in heaven and on earth'?
explanation: Matthew 28:18 — 'All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to me.'

content: Matthew opens with 'Immanuel' meaning 'God with us' (1:23). Which closing word (28:20) directly echoes this theme of 'God with us'?
explanation: Matt 1:23 and 28:20 form an 'inclusio': the book opens with 'Immanuel' (God with us) and closes with the promise 'I am with you always, to the end of the age'.
options: 'Behold, I am with you always, to the end of the age'
'All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to me'
'Go therefore and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them'
'You will receive the Holy Spirit not many days from now'

content: In Matthew 1-2, how many times in total does Joseph (Mary's husband) receive divine guidance through a dream?
explanation: Matthew records 4 dreams: 1:20 (take Mary), 2:13 (flee to Egypt), 2:19 (return to Israel), 2:22 (withdraw to Galilee).

content: In Matthew, the phrase 'If you are the Son of God, ___' is hurled at Jesus at least twice to test him. What are those two occasions?
explanation: Matt 4:3,6 — Satan tempts in the wilderness; 27:40 — passersby mock at the cross. Both use the same phrase 'If you are the Son of God'.
options: When he was arrested and when he stood before Pilate
During his dialogue with Nicodemus and in Gethsemane
In the wilderness temptation by Satan and on the cross by mockers
At the Last Supper and at the triumphal entry into Jerusalem

content: According to Matthew, Jesus predicted his Passion and resurrection to the disciples three distinct times. Which passages are among those three primary predictions? (select all correct)
explanation: The three Passion predictions: 16:21 (Caesarea Philippi), 17:22-23 (post-Transfiguration in Galilee), 20:17-19 (en route to Jerusalem). Matt 24:30 speaks of the Second Coming, not the Passion.
options: Matthew 16:21 — after Peter's confession at Caesarea Philippi
Matthew 17:22-23 — while traveling in Galilee after the Transfiguration
Matthew 20:17-19 — on the way up to Jerusalem
Matthew 24:30 — Olivet Discourse about the Son of Man's return

content: In Matthew 26:32 (Last Supper night), Jesus said, 'After I am raised up, I will go before you to Galilee.' Which passage records its fulfillment?
explanation: Matt 28:16-20 — 'the eleven disciples went to Galilee, to the mountain to which Jesus had directed them.' This is the Great Commission, fulfilling 26:32.
options: When he appeared to Mary Magdalene right at the empty tomb
When he walked with two disciples on the road to Emmaus
When he appeared among the disciples in a locked room in Jerusalem
When the eleven disciples came to Galilee, to the mountain he had appointed

content: In Matthew 5:13-16, the metaphors 'salt of the earth' and 'light of the world' share which structural parallel?
explanation: Matt 5:13 — salt that loses saltiness 'is no longer good for anything'. 5:14-16 — light must 'shine before others'. Both share: warning of lost function + call to public visibility.
options: Both warn of becoming useless if function is lost and place disciples in public roles
Both draw imagery from offering laws in the book of Leviticus
Both are tied directly to baptism and the Lord's Supper in the New Testament
Both apply only to the twelve apostles, not to the broader crowd of disciples

content: Jesus prophesied about the 'abomination of desolation' standing in the holy place (24:15). What specific event happened to the temple at the moment of his death on the cross (Matthew 27:51)?
explanation: Matt 27:51 — 'the curtain of the temple was torn in two, from top to bottom.' The direction 'top to bottom' marks it as God's act, opening the way into the Most Holy Place.
options: The temple was completely destroyed by Roman troops at that very moment
The curtain of the temple was torn in two, from top to bottom
The Pharisees seized the temple and shut its doors
A fire from heaven descended and burned up the altar inside the temple

content: Among the Kingdom parables in Matthew 13, which ones TEACH about the Kingdom's growth/spread from small to large? (select all correct)
explanation: 13:31-32 (mustard) and 13:33 (leaven) parallel each other in teaching the Kingdom's growth from small to vast. The wheat-and-weeds (13:24-30) teaches coexistence till harvest; the net (13:47-50) teaches final separation.
options: The mustard seed becoming a great tree (13:31-32)
The leaven making three measures of flour rise (13:33)
The wheat and the weeds growing together until harvest (13:24-30)
The net cast into the sea catching all kinds of fish (13:47-50)

content: After Jesus fed the 5,000 (Matthew 14) then the 4,000 (Matthew 15), how many baskets of leftovers were taken up, respectively?
explanation: Matt 14:20 — '12 baskets' (kophinos) after the 5,000. Matt 15:37 — '7 baskets' (spuridas, larger baskets) after the 4,000. Two different Greek words for two basket types.

content: Which blessing in the Sermon on the Mount (Matthew 5:3-12) promises that 'they shall inherit the earth'?
explanation: Matt 5:5 — 'Blessed are the meek, for they shall inherit the earth.' This echoes Psalm 37:11. Other Beatitudes: poor in spirit — Kingdom of Heaven; mourners — comfort; hunger for righteousness — they shall be filled.
options: Blessed are the meek of heart in this life
Blessed are the poor in spirit before God
Blessed are those who mourn over their sins
Blessed are those who hunger for righteousness

content: Matthew 22 records two religious groups trying to trap Jesus with two different questions. What did the Sadducees ask?
explanation: Matt 22:23-28 — Sadducees (denying the resurrection) posed the hypothetical 'woman with seven husbands' to refute resurrection. Pharisees asked about Caesar's tax (22:15-22) and the greatest commandment (22:34-40).
options: Whether or not it was lawful to pay taxes to Caesar
By what authority he was doing his works in the temple
About a woman married to seven brothers — whose wife at the resurrection
Which is the greatest commandment in all the Law of Moses

content: In the list of the twelve apostles in Matthew 10:2-4, which person is given a specific alternative descriptor? (select all correct)
explanation: Matt 10:3-4 — 'Matthew the tax collector', 'Thaddaeus' (some MSS Lebbaeus), 'Simon the Cananaean' (Hebrew qana — zealous). Peter has 'who is called Peter' replacing Simon's name (10:2) — not an additional alias.
options: Matthew — explicitly described as 'the tax collector'
Peter — listed as brother of Andrew (no extra alias attached)
Simon — called 'the Cananaean' meaning the Zealot
Thaddaeus — also known as Lebbaeus in some manuscripts

content: Matthew 1-2 quotes Old Testament prophecies fulfilled in Jesus' birth. Which of these is one of those quotations? (select all correct)
explanation: Matt 1:23 quotes Isa 7:14, 2:6 quotes Mic 5:2, 2:15 quotes Hos 11:1, 2:18 quotes Jer 31:15. Samuel anointing David is not a Nativity-related prophecy.
options: A virgin shall conceive and bear a son (Isaiah 7:14)
A ruler shall come forth from Bethlehem (Micah 5:2)
Out of Egypt I called my son (Hosea 11:1)
Samuel anointing David as king (1 Samuel 16)

content: Both judgment scenes in Matthew 7:21-23 and 25:31-46 contain 'Depart from me'. What is the core difference between them?
explanation: Matt 7:21-23 — Christ rejects those who say 'Lord, Lord', who prophesy and cast out demons, for 'not doing the Father's will'. 25:31-46 — judges all nations by their treatment of 'the least of these' (hungry, thirsty, stranger, naked, sick, prisoner).
options: Scene 7 targets Gentiles while scene 25 targets Israel alone
Scene 7 takes place in heaven while scene 25 takes place on earth among men
Scene 7 condemns self-proclaimed disciples doing miracles; scene 25 condemns indifference to the needy
Scene 7 applies only to Pharisees, scene 25 only to Sadducees

content: Matthew 5:48 — 'You therefore must be perfect, as your heavenly Father is perfect'. According to its immediate context (5:43-47), Jesus illustrates this 'perfection' specifically by what?
explanation: Matt 5:43-48 — Father-like 'perfection' is illustrated by loving enemies: 'he makes his sun rise on the evil and on the good'. This caps the six antitheses of 5:21-48.
options: Keeping all the ritual purity laws of Moses
Fasting forty days as he did in the wilderness
Withdrawing from public life for continual prayer
Loving one's enemies and praying for those who persecute

content: Matthew 6:33 — 'But seek first the kingdom of God and his righteousness'. In context (6:25-34), what theological priority does this teach?
explanation: Matt 6:25-34 contrasts material anxiety with trust in the Father. 6:33 sets the priority: seek the Kingdom first, and 'all these things' will be added.
options: Place the Kingdom and righteousness above all material concerns, trusting the Father's provision
Forsake all property and live as a hermit like later monastic communities
First pay taxes in full to Caesar before any offering to God
Attend every temple festival before any other activity in life

content: In Matthew 7:13-14, Jesus teaches about the 'narrow gate' and the 'wide gate'. According to the text itself, what is the core theological reason for choosing the narrow gate?
explanation: Matt 7:13-14 — 'wide is the gate and broad is the way that leads to destruction, and many enter; narrow is the gate and hard is the way that leads to life, and few find it.' Choice rests on eternal destiny, not present advantage.
options: Because the narrow gate leads to material prosperity in this present life
Because the narrow gate leads to life, while the wide gate leads to destruction
Because the narrow gate is easier to find, and many find their way through it
Because the narrow gate avoids the persecution of the Pharisees of that time

content: Matthew 9:13 — Jesus quotes Hosea 6:6 'I desire mercy, not sacrifice' to defend which action of his?
explanation: Matt 9:9-13 — Pharisees rebuke Jesus for eating with tax collectors (Matthew and his friends). Jesus answers with Hos 6:6, teaching that compassion for sinners outweighs ritual.
options: Healing a blind man on the Sabbath in front of a crowd
Eating bread without washing hands according to elders' tradition
Driving out the merchants from the temple in Jerusalem
Sitting at table eating with tax collectors and sinners

content: Matthew 12:8 — Jesus declares: 'The Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath'. What does this claim about his authority imply?
explanation: Matt 12:1-8 — Pharisees rebuke disciples for plucking grain on the Sabbath. Jesus answers with David eating consecrated bread and priests' Sabbath labor in the temple. 'Lord of the Sabbath' = supreme interpretive authority, not abolition.
options: He has supreme authority to rightly interpret and apply the Sabbath law
He has completely abolished the Sabbath; disciples need not keep it any longer
He only has authority to heal on the Sabbath day, nothing else
He has instituted a wholly new Sabbath day distinct from the old one

content: Matthew 16:24 — 'If anyone would come after me, let him deny himself and take up his cross and follow me'. According to the text, in what order do these three steps occur?
explanation: Matt 16:24 sets the order clearly: (1) deny self — foundational disposition, (2) take up the cross — accept death to self, (3) follow me — lifelong walk. 16:25 gives the reason: 'lose your life to save it'.
options: Take up the cross → deny self → follow Christ (outward action first)
Deny self → take up the cross → follow Christ (inner attitude, then action, then walk)
Follow Christ → deny self → take up the cross (following precedes the call to sacrifice)
All three happen simultaneously without any specific order in the text

content: When asked 'which is the greatest commandment' (Matthew 22:36), what unique answer-structure did Jesus give?
explanation: Matt 22:37-40 — Jesus states the greatest commandment (love God with all heart, soul, mind — Deut 6:5) AND adds 'a second is like it: love your neighbor as yourself' (Lev 19:18). 'On these two commandments depend all the Law and the Prophets.'
options: A single commandment only: love God with all your soul
He summarized the Ten Commandments into one single golden rule
The FIRST commandment (love God) AND a second 'like the first' (love neighbor)
A list of four commandments ranked in descending order of importance

content: Matthew 27:46 — Jesus cried out from the cross: 'Eli, Eli, lema sabachthani?' What theological and literary significance does this carry? (select all correct)
explanation: Matt 27:46 quotes Ps 22:1 (A) — a psalm beginning in agony but ending in triumph (22:22-31). Doctrinally (B), Christ bore sin's penalty and was forsaken by the Father in his people's stead. The crowd MISUNDERSTANDS (C, D) thinking he calls for Elijah (27:47), but he does not literally plead for rescue.
options: It is a direct quotation of Psalm 22:1, a psalm of suffering ending in vindication
It expresses the truth that Christ bore sin and was forsaken by the Father in his people's place
It is a plea for the disciples to come and rescue him from the cross in that moment
It is an allusion that the prophet Elijah will descend and save him from death

content: Matthew 18:20 — 'For where two or three are gathered in my name, there am I among them'. According to its immediate context (18:15-19), this promise specifically applies to what situation?
explanation: Matt 18:15-20 addresses church discipline: a brother sins → confront privately → bring 1-2 witnesses → tell the church. 'Two or three' (18:20) primarily references the witnesses and church judgment, secondarily extending to united prayer (18:19).
options: Every religious worship service across all the great world religions
Two or three friends gathered at a coffee shop discussing spiritual things in general
Any spontaneous home group with no connection to any organized church
The process of church discipline and the unified prayer of the church body

content: According to Matthew 3:17 (ESV), what was the voice from heaven at Jesus' baptism?
explanation: Matt 3:17 — 'This is my beloved Son, with whom I am well pleased.' 'Listen to him' is the voice at the Transfiguration (17:5), DISTINCT from the baptism. 'Today I have begotten you' is Ps 2:7 (Luke has variant, not Matthew). Peter's confession 16:16 is also distinct.
options: 'This is my beloved Son, with whom I am well pleased'
'Today I have begotten you; ask of me, and I will give you the nations'
'Listen to him, this is my beloved Son'
'You are the Messiah, the Son of the living God'

content: In the Lord's Prayer of Matthew 6:9-13 (ESV), what is the order of the FIRST THREE petitions (after the address 'Our Father in heaven')?
explanation: Matt 6:9-10 — '1) Hallowed be your name. 2) Your kingdom come. 3) Your will be done, on earth as it is in heaven.' These three God-ward petitions precede the four man-ward petitions (6:11-13).
options: Give us this day our daily bread → forgive our debts → lead us not into temptation
Hallowed be your name → your kingdom come → your will be done on earth as in heaven
Forgive us our debts → as we forgive our debtors → deliver us from evil
For yours is the kingdom and power and glory → hallowed be your name → kingdom come

content: Matthew 11:29-30 (ESV) — Jesus invites: 'Take my yoke upon you and learn from me...' According to the text itself, what reason does he give for this invitation?
explanation: Matt 11:29-30 — '...for I am gentle and lowly in heart, and you will find rest for your souls. For my yoke is easy, and my burden is light.' Gentleness + humility ground the invitation, contrasted with the scribes' burdensome yoke.
options: Because I will grant you authority over demonic powers throughout the earth
Because I am the strictest teacher among all the rabbis and Pharisees in the land
Because I am gentle and lowly in heart; my yoke is easy and my burden is light
Because you have witnessed many of my miracles recently in Capernaum

content: Matthew 22:21 — Jesus answers about Caesar's tax: 'Therefore render to ___ the things that are ___'s, and to ___ the things that are ___'s'. What two name-pairs fill the blanks in order?
explanation: Matt 22:21 — 'Render to Caesar the things that are Caesar's, and to God the things that are God's.' The reply balances civic duty with duty to God.
options: Temple / temple's; high priest / high priest's

content: According to Matthew 26:26-28, when instituting the Lord's Supper, what did Jesus say over the CUP after giving thanks?
explanation: Matt 26:27-28 — 'Drink of it, all of you, for this is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many for the forgiveness of sins.' The text stresses the NEW COVENANT and forgiveness for 'many'.
options: 'Drink of it; this is my blood of the covenant, poured out for many for forgiveness of sins'
'This is the cup of the first covenant which Moses sprinkled on the people with bulls' blood'
'This is the blood of the Passover lamb; drink it as a perpetual memorial of Israel's Exodus'
'Keep this sacred cup safely until you enter the Kingdom of God's everlasting people'

content: Matthew 28:18 — before the Great Commission, what does Jesus declare as the basis for the command to go and disciple the nations?
explanation: Matt 28:18 — 'All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to me.' The claim of FULL authority is the BASIS for the command to go (28:19) and the promise of presence (28:20).
options: 'The Father has given me a measure of authority in heaven, enough for your preaching'
'All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to me'
'The Holy Spirit will grant you authority not many days from now'
'Authority comes from your unified prayers gathered together'

content: After Jesus breathed his last and the earth shook (Matthew 27:54), what did the centurion and the soldiers guarding him say?
explanation: Matt 27:54 — 'When the centurion and those who were with him, keeping watch over Jesus, saw the earthquake and what took place, they were filled with awe and said, Truly this was the Son of God!' This is a Gentile's confession.
options: 'Truly this man was the King of the Jews as written on the cross'
'We have killed a righteous man; we ourselves have erred'
'Truly this man indeed was the Son of God'
'This was a great prophet sent by God to Israel today'
